scale.intro: Each criterion is scored on a scale from 0 to 5. The higher the score, the better the creative aligns with professional standards. Descriptions are based on knowledge-base rules and thresholds used in automated analysis.

sources.title: J. Felici, Typography: Type, Layout, and Design
R. Bringhurst, The Elements of Typographic Style
T. Stone et al., Color Design Workbook
T. Makarova, N. Samsonova, The Role of Color in Advertising Imagery
J. Müller-Brockmann, Grid Systems in Graphic Design
S. Krug, Don't Make Me Think

levels.title: Absent
levels.description: The layout has no text, or typography does not serve a communicative function: labels are decorative and illegible, or text is missing where the brief requires it.

levels.title: Critical
levels.description: Text-to-background contrast is below 3:1, type size is too small for the display channel, or more than three typefaces are used without a system. Text is hard to read at a glance.

levels.title: Poor
levels.description: Weak contrast, no clear size hierarchy (less than 1.5× difference), excessive or insufficient line spacing. Typefaces clash in character.

levels.title: Satisfactory
levels.description: Text is readable on close inspection. Moderate contrast, basic headline/body hierarchy exists, but without precise typographic discipline.

levels.title: Good
levels.description: Contrast ≥ 4.5:1, one to two typefaces, clear hierarchy of sizes and weights. Letter-spacing and line spacing are aligned with text volume.

levels.title: Excellent
levels.description: Typography is built on a modular grid: high contrast, hierarchy is instantly clear, the type pairing is harmonious, and text reads easily on the target medium.

levels.title: Absent
levels.description: Color is not used meaningfully: random palette, no link to brand or brief, monochrome without accents where they are needed.

levels.title: Critical
levels.description: More than six dominant colors, saturation > 75% without balance, mixed harmony—the palette looks chaotic and fatiguing.

levels.title: Poor
levels.description: Palette is washed out (saturation < 15%) or excessively loud. Colors are not coordinated on the Itten wheel, no functional accent.

levels.title: Satisfactory
levels.description: Two to four main colors, analogous or neutral palette. Medium saturation. An accent exists, but its role is not always obvious.

levels.title: Good
levels.description: Complementary or triadic harmony, saturation 30–60%, each color has a role (background, text, CTA). Palette fits the advertising context.

levels.title: Excellent
levels.description: Palette is refined: harmonious Itten-based combination, color semantics support the brand message, accent guides attention without overload.

levels.title: Absent
levels.description: All elements are equal in weight: no primary message, the eye finds no entry point, the layout reads as noise.

levels.title: Critical
levels.description: No focus: zone activity is even (focus ratio < 1.2), contrast is low, the headline is not distinguished by size or color.

levels.title: Poor
levels.description: Weak focus, several elements compete for attention. Not enough whitespace around key blocks.

levels.title: Satisfactory
levels.description: A main element exists, but secondary items are not differentiated by contrast and size. Reading order takes effort.

levels.title: Good
levels.description: Clear visual anchor (focus ratio ≥ 2), contrast supports priorities. Headline → image → body read in sequence.

levels.title: Excellent
levels.description: Hierarchy is built through size, contrast, color, and space. The eye path is natural; the CTA is noticeable without aggression.

levels.title: Absent
levels.description: Elements are scattered without structure: no grid, balance, or placement logic; the layout looks accidental.

levels.title: Critical
levels.description: Strong imbalance (brightness difference between halves > 25%), zone overload, basic proportions and margins violated.

levels.title: Poor
levels.description: Moderate imbalance (10–25), elements stick to edges, rule of thirds unused, no rhythm.

levels.title: Satisfactory
levels.description: Balance is acceptable (< 10), but composition is generic. Basic alignment exists, yet no expressive center.

levels.title: Good
levels.description: Balanced composition using rule of thirds or modular grid. Active zones are distributed purposefully; frame proportions fit.

levels.title: Excellent
levels.description: Composition guides the eye; balance is symmetric or asymmetric by intent. Elements align to a modular system.
